etymology.description: semantic 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 庚(庚) -> integrative element -> final form was 唐
etymology.interpretation: The character 唐(táng) originally referred to 'exaggerate, boast' with the mouth being the indicative semantic component 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") and 庚(庚) serving as the phonetic element. The transformation of the phonetic component indicates a shift over time from its more complete original form to the current contracted version. In the historical context, this base sense was related to expressions considered grand or impressive, leading to the Tang Dynasty being associated with grandness or magnificence, such as in 唐诗 ('Tang poetry'), distinguished for its literary excellence.

parts.english: standard; fifth of the ten heavenly stems

examples.chinese: 唐朝是一個文化繁榮的時期(唐朝是一個文化繁榮的時期)。
examples.pinyin: Tángcháo shì yī gè wénhuà fánróng de shíqí.
examples.english: The Tang dynasty was a period of cultural prosperity.

examples.chinese: 唐代詩歌極富創意(唐代詩歌極富創意)，許多詩人留下不朽之作(許多詩人留下不朽之作)。
examples.pinyin: Tángdài shīgē jí fù chuàngyì, xǔduō shīrén liúxià bùxiǔ zhī zuò.
examples.english: Poetry from the Tang dynasty was extremely creative, and many poets left behind immortal works.

examples.chinese: 許多留學生對唐文化感興趣(許多留學生對唐文化感興趣)，因為它影響了整個東亞地區(因為它影響了整個東亞地區)。
examples.pinyin: Xǔduō liúxuéshēng duì Táng wénhuà gǎn xìngqù, yīnwèi tā yǐngxiǎngle zhěng gè Dōng Yà dìqū.
examples.english: Many international students are interested in Tang culture because it influenced the entire East Asian region.
